European governments are growing uneasy with Mark Rutte because he has been publicly supportive of Trump’s aggressive moves, including the Iran strikes, even when many European leaders strongly disagreed. His strategy of flattering Trump and echoing his rhetoric has given him direct access and some influence, but it’s now being seen as too deferential and potentially undermining Europe’s own position. Source: Bloomberg

Status-6 (War & Military News) A video showing fire on the territory of the Lavan refinery situated on Iran's Lavan Island in the Persian Gulf. The National Iranian ​Oil Refining ​and Distribution Company said that the fire was caused by an "enemy attack," without specifying who was responsible for it. Iran’s state broadcaster later reported that Iranian forces launched series of strikes against the United Arab Emirates and Kuwait as a response to the attack on the Lavan refinery. tweet

OSINTdefender According to the United Arab Emirates (UAE) Ministry of Defense, on April 8th, the UAE’s air defenses engaged 17 Iranian ballistic missiles and 35 Iranian drones. 3 people sustained minor injuries in the strikes. The total tally of Iranian strikes on the UAE is now 2,256 drones, 26 cruise missiles, and 537 ballistic missiles. The UAE has borne the brunt of Iranian missile and drone strikes on its Gulf neighbors. tweet

OSINTdefender Iranian state news outlets are blaming the U.S. and Israel for recent strikes on the oil refinery on Lavan Island. No word from the Israel Defense Force or CENTCOM at the time of writing https://twitter.com/sentdefender/status/2041833927237685470/photo/1 tweet

Notably, the strike targeted Sirri Island, where, similar to Kharg Island, key oil facilities are located. This appears to be a signal that if Iran continues its attacks on Arab states, they may—unlike the Americans—target Kharg Island itself, which handles around 90% of Iran’s oil exports. 🇮🇷 Iran’s Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) reported on last night's “100th wave” of Operation True Promise 4, calling it a direct response to recent attacks on Iranian infrastructure. It said it does not trust Western promises and warned that any further aggression will be met with stronger retaliation. According to the IRGC, more than 25 strategic targets were hit across the region—from U.S. positions in the Middle East to key sites inside Israel—using ballistic missiles, cruise missiles, and drones. Targets reportedly included major energy infrastructure linked to U.S. companies such as Chevron and ExxonMobil across Saudi Arabia, Qatar, the UAE, Bahrain, and Kuwait, including oil refineries, petrochemical plants, and pipeline networks bypassing the Strait of Hormuz. Inside Israel, the IRGC claims strikes on the Haifa oil refinery, Ben Gurion Airport, intelligence and surveillance centers, government command facilities, and advanced technology sites in Tel Aviv and Be’er Sheva. The statement also claims attacks on U.S. military assets, including bases in Jordan and Kuwait, as well as naval forces operating in regional waters. The IRGC said the operation stretched from the Mediterranean to the eastern Arabian Peninsula, delivering heavy blows to enemy infrastructure, and warned that its forces remain ready to escalate further if provoked again. 🔴 @DDGeopolitics

🇮🇷 ⚡️ IRGC: The enemy has always deceived us, and we have no trust in its promises. We will respond to any aggression at a higher level. Last night, with divine grace and the support of the Iranian nation, the IRGC and Basij forces carried out the 100th wave of Operation True Promise 4 (code name: Ya Fatimah al-Zahra). They struck more than 25 strategic targets, including 13 energy complexes and oil lines linked to America and the Zionist regime, plus 10 military, security, and logistical targets. The Aerospace Force hit enemy targets from the Mediterranean shores to eastern Hijaz in honor of the martyred Leader Imam Khamenei. The IRGC remains fully prepared under the command of Ayatollah Sayyed Mojtaba Khamenei , hands on the trigger, ready for an even greater response if the enemy miscalculates again. The enemy always lies - we trust none of its promises and will answer any attack with greater force.
